message.content: {"items":[{"title":"ALLBUS/GGSS 1991 – Allgemeine Bevölkerungsumfrage der Sozialwissenschaften","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2259","justification":"German General Social Survey including historical attitudes and retrospective questions; focuses on Germany and covers respondents born in the 1950s–1970s, thus reflecting historical developments in post‑war Germany."},{"title":"ALLBUS/GGSS 1998 – Allgemeine Bevölkerungsumfrage der Sozialwissenschaften","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2257","justification":"German General Social Survey with modules on historical memory and political history in Germany, capturing experiences and evaluations of developments from the 1950s onward."},{"title":"ALLBUS/GGSS 2000 – Religion and Church in Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12303","justification":"Focuses on religion and church but includes historical context and retrospective items related to social and political developments in Germany since the 1950s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Role of Government II – ISSP 1990","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12282","justification":"Cross‑national dataset including Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA; covers views on the role of government and includes items tied to post‑war historical developments, especially 1950s–1980s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Role of Government III – ISSP 1996","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12283","justification":"Includes Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA; captures attitudes to government in light of Cold War and post‑Cold‑War historical changes from the 1950s–1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: National Identity I – ISSP 1995","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12294","justification":"Contains questions on national history, pride in historical achievements, and views on historical events; includes Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: National Identity II – ISSP 2003","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12295","justification":"Builds on the 1995 National Identity module, with items about historical events, national history, and evaluations of past periods; includes Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA, spanning post‑1950 developments."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Religion I – ISSP 1991","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12305","justification":"Historical change in religiosity and church–state relations in Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA, with questions reflecting transformations since the 1950s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Social Inequality I – ISSP 1987","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12289","justification":"Examines perceptions of inequality and mobility in a historical context; participating countries include Germany, Great Britain, Sweden, and the USA, capturing experiences rooted in the 1950s–1980s."},{"title":"Comparative Study of Electoral Systems (CSES) Module 1 – GESIS Archive Version","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12298","justification":"Post‑war electoral behavior and party system development in multiple democracies including Germany, Great Britain/UK, Sweden, and the USA; voters’ behavior and attitudes are shaped by historical trajectories from the 1950s–2000s."}]}
